items.back: Device containing NO dangerous materials but closely resembling nuclear or explosive items classified as dangerous — could be easily mistaken for dangerous counterparts.

items.front: 9-2-1: Two things controller advises when amending route/altitude for dangerous cargo flight
items.back: 1) The proposed change. 2) The amount of delay to expect if it is necessary to maintain the present route/altitude.

items.front: 9-2-1: Pilot refuses clearance amendment — what pilot tells ATC and what ATC does
items.back: Pilot advises if traffic delay is acceptable or if alternate route/altitude desired. ATC offers all possible assistance.

items.front: 9-2-2: Lateral limit for celestial navigation training
items.back: Within 30 NM of the route centerline specified in the en route clearance, unless otherwise authorized by ATC.

items.front: 9-2-2: Heading change notification threshold during celestial nav training
items.back: Pilot must advise ATC before initiating any heading changes exceeding 20 degrees.

items.front: 9-2-2: Two requirements for celestial nav training in conterminous U.S.
items.back: Transponder-equipped aircraft AND within areas of ARTCC radar coverage.

items.front: 9-2-3: Two conditions for clearing experimental aircraft with special handling
items.back: Clear according to pilot requests: 1) as traffic permits, and 2) if not contrary to ATC procedures.

items.front: 9-2-3: When can controller ask experimental aircraft to deviate from approved plan?
items.back: Only to preclude an emergency situation. Once approved, do not ask the pilot to deviate from planned action otherwise.

items.front: 9-2-4: Two preconditions for approving FAA R&D special flight procedures
items.back: 1) Coordinated in advance. 2) Traffic permits. Both required.

items.front: 9-2-4: Who approves special flight procedures for FAA R&D test activities?
items.back: The facility air traffic manager, prior to their use.
Aircraft transporting nuclear emergency support team or other disaster response teams to potential/actual nuclear, radiological, chemical, or hazardous materials incident.

items.front: 9-2-5: First facility actions for FLYNET aircraft (3 items)
items.back: 1) Provide expeditious handling. 2) Report to OS/CIC for reporting to NTSO ATSC through DEN. 3) Add DEN notification to flight plan remarks.

items.front: 9-2-6: What info to request from pilot before MTR entry?
items.back: Estimate for exit/alternate exit fix, requested altitude after exit, and if applicable, number of reentries on Strategic Training Range.

items.front: 9-2-6: MARSA on MTRs — what must LOA cover?
items.back: Appropriate MARSA application to preclude inadvertent compromise of MARSA standards by ATC.

items.front: 9-2-6: IR two-way radio failure — transponder procedure
items.back: 7700 during climb/descent to filed altitude; then 7600 for 15 min, 7700 for 1 min at end of each 15-min period, 7600 at all other times.

items.front: 9-2-7: Duration of max assistance for interceptor aircraft on scramble
items.back: Until the unknown aircraft is identified per FAA Order JO 7610.4 policies and procedures.

items.front: 9-2-7: ADCF scramble requirement for active air defense mission
items.back: Must identify the mission as an active air defense mission.
